quality.rationale: 基于无穷小平行四边形构造的李导数几何意义证明，逻辑链条完整且具有微分流形通用性

origin.question: Problem 8.14. Suppose $\mathbf{A} = \mathrm{d} / \mathrm{d}\lambda$ is the tangent vector field to a congruence (set of curves) $\mathbf{x}^{\alpha} = \mathbf{x}^{\alpha}(\lambda)$ and $\mathbf{B}$ is a vector field. Show that the geometrical interpretation of the transport law $\mathfrak{L}_{\mathbf{A}}\mathbf{B} = \mathbf{0}$ is that $\mathbf{B}$ connects points of equal $\lambda$ on neighboring curves of the congruence.
origin.answer: $\mathbf{K} = [\mathbf{A}, \mathbf{B}] = \mathcal{L}_{\mathbf{A}}\mathbf{B}$，故 $\mathcal{L}_{\mathbf{A}}\mathbf{B} = \mathbf{0}$ 等价于 $\mathbf{B}$ 连接相邻曲线的等 $\lambda$ 点。
origin.solution: Solution 8.14.![](images/7c8f21261efc33744042bc400d815ff14261c28fe92d8ad76dc680dbcd709f7f.jpg)At the points $\mathbf{P}_0$ and $\mathbf{P}_1$ consider two tangent vectors $\mathbf{A}(\mathbf{P}_0)$ and $\mathbf{A}(\mathbf{P}_1)$ each extending from $\lambda = 0$ to $\lambda = \Delta \lambda = \mathcal{O}(\epsilon)$ . (Note: In the figure $\Delta \lambda$ is taken to be unity so that the $\mathbf{A}$ vectors are large and the "difference vector" $\mathbf{K}$ is exaggerated for clarity.) Suppose that at point $\mathbf{P}_1$ the vector $\mathbf{B}(\mathbf{P}_1)$ connects $\mathbf{P}_1$ and $\mathbf{P}_0$ . Let us compute the amount by which the vector field $\mathbf{B}$ fails to connect points of equal $\lambda$ along the congruence $\mathbf{A}$ , i.e. let us compute the vector $\mathbf{K}$ :$$\begin{array}{l} \mathbf {K} = \left[ \mathbf {B} \left(\mathbf {P}_{1}\right) + \mathbf {A} \left(\mathbf {P}_{0}\right)\right] - \left[ \mathbf {A} \left(\mathbf {P}_{1}\right) + \mathbf {B} \left(\mathbf {P}_{3}\right)\right] \\ = \left[ \mathbf {B} \left(\mathbf {P}_{1}\right) - \mathbf {B} \left(\mathbf {P}_{3}\right)\right] - \left[ \mathbf {A} \left(\mathbf {P}_{1}\right) - \mathbf {A} \left(\mathbf {P}_{0}\right)\right] \\ = \mathrm {A}^{\tau} \mathrm {B}^{\gamma},_{\tau} \mathbf {e}_{\gamma} + \mathcal {O} (\mathrm {A}^{2} \mathrm {B}) - \mathrm {B}^{\tau} \mathrm {A}^{\gamma},_{\tau} \mathbf {e}_{\gamma} + \mathcal {O} (\mathrm {B}^{2} \mathrm {A}) \\\\ = [ \mathbf {A}, \mathbf {B} ] + \mathcal {O} (\epsilon^{3}) \text {i f} | \mathbf {A} | \sim \mathcal {O} (\epsilon) \sim | \mathbf {B} |. \\\end{array}$$Thus in the limit of infinitesimal vectors A and B,$$\mathbf {K} = \left[ \begin{array}{c c} \mathbf {A}, \mathbf {B} \end{array}\right] = \mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {A}} \mathbf {B}.$$Thus $\mathbf{K}$ vanishes if $\mathcal{L}_{\mathbf{A}}\mathbf{B} = 0$ , i.e. if $\mathbf{B}$ is “Lie-dragged” along $\mathbf{A}$ .
